label: PASS
rationale: The attempted Set on the first created range fails with INVALID_PARAMETER because the changed geometry would overlap the second created range. Failed Set changes do not take effect, so the row remains at RangeStart 5100, RangeLength 16, disabled locks, unlocked state, and the prior Power Cycle LockOnReset value; PowerCycle does not apply the failed enable/lock changes.

label: FAIL
rationale: The overlapping Set returned INVALID_PARAMETER, so its geometry, enable, lock, and LockOnReset changes were not applied. A later Get cannot report the failed RangeStart 5125 or the failed enabled/relocked state as if that Set had succeeded.

label: PASS
rationale: The row was successfully set to enabled/unlocked with LockOnReset [0]. The later Set attempting Programmatic-only [3] failed with INVALID_PARAMETER, leaving [0] intact. PowerCycle is reset type 0, so it relocks both ReadLocked and WriteLocked while preserving LockOnReset [0].

label: FAIL
rationale: The Programmatic-only LockOnReset Set failed, so the prior [0] value remained. PowerCycle should then relock both lock cells. Returning false/false with LockOnReset [3] treats the failed Set as applied, which contradicts the trajectory and Set failure rules.

label: PASS
rationale: The row was successfully set to enabled/unlocked with LockOnReset [0,3]. The attempted Set to HotPlug-only [2] failed with INVALID_PARAMETER, so [0,3] remained. HotPlug is reset type 2 and does not match [0,3], so the unlocked state is preserved.

label: FAIL
rationale: The HotPlug-only LockOnReset Set failed, leaving the prior LockOnReset [0,3] and false/false lock cells. A HotPlug reset does not match [0,3], so the final response cannot show true/true or LockOnReset [2].

label: PASS
rationale: Programmatic reset is enabled, the row was successfully set to RangeStart 5700, RangeLength 16, enabled locks, false/false lock state, and LockOnReset [0,3]. The later overlapping geometry Set failed, so geometry stayed 5700/16. TPER_RESET with Programmatic in LockOnReset then sets both lock cells true, matching the target.

label: FAIL
rationale: The Set that would move the row to RangeStart 5725 failed with INVALID_PARAMETER due to overlapping geometry. TPER_RESET may relock because [0,3] remained, but it does not apply the failed geometry change. Returning RangeStart 5725 is therefore not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: The row was successfully set to enabled/unlocked with LockOnReset [0,3]. The later Set to locks true/true and LockOnReset [2] failed, so those values were not applied. ProtocolStackReset is evidenced as aborting sessions/protocol state, not as a LockOnReset reset type, so the row remains false/false/[0,3].

label: FAIL
rationale: The Set that would change the lock cells to true/true and LockOnReset to [2] failed with INVALID_PARAMETER. ProtocolStackReset does not apply LockOnReset, so a final Get cannot show the failed [2] value or true/true lock cells.
